description: Inside a busy coffee shop, Ross, Monica, Joey, and Chandler sit around a couch and table. Phoebe walks past the counter, where Rachel is working, and approaches the group holding a denim jacket. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe says, "Hi, guys." Joey says, "Hey, baby." Ross asks, "Hey. Oh, how'd it go?" Rachel pours coffee into a mug.
Phoebe answers, "Not so good. He walked me to the subway and said, we should do this again."
Rachel stops pouring and looks up at Phoebe, her eyes wide.
Rachel asks, "He said, we should do it again? That's good, right?"
Monica gestures with her hand and replies, "No. Loosely translated, we should do this again means you will never see me naked." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel lowers the coffee pot and stares at Monica.
Rachel asks, "Since when?" Joey answers, "Since always. It's like dating language." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures with his mug and says, "Like, It's not you means, It is you."
Chandler, sitting on the floor next to Monica, adds, "Or you're such a nice guy means, I'm going to be dating leather wearing"
Chandler continues, "alcoholics and complaining about them to you." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe holds a pink mug, looking upward.
Phoebe says, "I think we should see other people. Mean, I already am"
Rachel stands near the group holding a mug. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel asks, "and everybody knows that." Joey replies, "Oh, yeah."
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "Cushions the blow." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "Yeah. It's like when you're a kid and your parents put your dog to sleep and they tell you it went off to live on some farm. That's"
Ross points at Chandler and says, "funny. No, because our parents actually did send our dog off to live on a farm."
Monica looks at Ross, her mouth slightly open. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica says, "Ross?"
Ross looks back at Monica, smiling slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross says, "Hello. The Milners Farm in Connecticut. The Milners."
Ross continues, "They had this unbelievable farm that they had horses and then rabbits that he could"
Ross continues, "chase. And it" while looking around the quiet group. Audience laughter begins.
Ross's facial expression falls neutral as he stares straight ahead, the others watching him silently.
Ross touches his forehead and says, "Oh, my God. Chi Chi."
Ross sits frozen with his hand near his head as the others look at him in silence. Audience laughter continues.
The view goes completely black.
Outside, tall brick buildings line a city street. The setting changes to an apartment living room where Chandler holds a script, standing near Joey who sits in a chair.
Chandler says, "So how does it feel knowing you're about to die?"
Joey nods.
Joey holds up his index finger and says, "Warden, in five minutes my pain will be over."
Joey continues, "But you'll have to live with the knowledge that you sent an honest man"
Joey points his finger at Chandler and says, "to die."
Chandler raises his eyebrows.
Chandler says, "Hey, that was really good." Joey asks, "Yeah?"
Chandler nods.
Joey smiles and says, "Thanks. Let's keep going."
Chandler looks at his script and says, "Okay. So, what do you want from me, Demone?"
Joey says, "Huh?"
Joey leans forward slightly, looking up at Chandler. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures and says, "I just want to go back to my cell because in my cell I can smoke." Chandler says, "Smoke away."
Joey pulls a pack of cigarettes and a lighter from his pocket, places a cigarette in his mouth, and attempts to light it. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey fumbles and drops the lighter and the cigarette onto his lap. Chandler places his hands on his head. Audience laughter continues.
Joey retrieves the items, places the cigarette back in his mouth, successfully lights it, and blows out a cloud of smoke.
Chandler leans down toward Joey and says, "I think this is probably why Demone smokes in his cell alone." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures with the hand holding the cigarette. Chandler gestures back at him.
Chandler says, "Relax your hand."
Chandler says, "Let your wrist go."
Chandler gently touches Joey's wrist.
Chandler says, "Not so much."
Joey's wrist drops limply. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "Oh,"
Chandler adjusts Joey's hand position.
Chandler says, "all right. I'll try taking a puff."
Joey brings the cigarette to his mouth.
Joey says, "Right."
Joey takes a puff and exhales the smoke while coughing slightly, his mouth pulled to the side. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler reaches for the cigarette and says, "Okay. No, give it to me." Joey pulls his hand away and says, "No, I'm not giving you a cigarette."
Chandler gestures with open hands and says, "It's fine. Look, do you want to get this part or not?"
Joey hands the cigarette to Chandler, saying, "Here." Chandler says, "All right."
Chandler stands up, holding the cigarette up to eye level, and says, "Now, don't think of it as a cigarette."
Chandler continues, "Think of it as the thing that has been missing from your hand."
Chandler continues to gaze at the cigarette.
Chandler says, "When you're holding it, you feel right. You feel complete." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey asks, "You miss it?" Chandler answers quickly, "No, not so much."
Chandler lowers his head and places a hand over his eyes. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler straightens up and says, "All right. Now we smoke."
Chandler places the cigarette in his mouth, takes a long drag, removes it, and exhales the smoke upward with his eyes closed. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler leans back slightly, eyes still closed, and says, "Oh, my God."
Chandler lowers his head and covers his face with his hand, dropping into a slight crouch. Audience laughter continues.
Joey stands up and reaches toward Chandler, saying, "Chandler. Give me."
Chandler backs away, gesturing with the cigarette, and says, "No, no. You got your option. You can smoke it like this." Joey steps toward him, saying, "Chandler."

Chandler moves around the room and says, "Or you can just hold it in your mouth and smoke it like this." Joey says, "Give me" while continuing to reach for the cigarette. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey says, "this cigarette. Give me. Give me." Chandler hands the cigarette to Joey, saying, "All right, you try." Joey replies, "Thank you."
Joey takes a puff and asks, "Okay, how's this?"
Joey holds the cigarette stiffly to his mouth as Chandler stares at him with an unmoving face. Audience laughter is audible.
In a kitchen, Chandler and Joey stand near a refrigerator. Joey holds an unlit cigarette.
"Okay, that's not bad," Chandler says to Joey.
"All right," Chandler adds.
"Now, when you're finished, it's really cool if you flick it. You kind of flick it," Chandler says, demonstrating a flicking motion with his thumb and index finger.
"You flick it," Chandler repeats as Joey mimics the flicking motion.
Joey looks down at his hand while still holding the cigarette.
Chandler pats Joey's shoulder and says, "That's good. All right. You keep practicing, and I'll go put out the sofa." Chandler walks away, leaving Joey standing alone.
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Through the window, the group is visible sitting around a coffee table. Instrumental music plays briefly. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler, sitting with his feet propped on the table, says, "I thought it had to do with the size of a guy's feet."
Monica gestures with her thumb and index finger extended as she says, "No, they say it's the same as the distance from the tip of a guy's thumb to the tip of his index finger."
Ross, Joey, and Chandler all raise their hands, extending their thumbs and index fingers to examine the distance. Audience laughter is audible.
They continue staring at their hands in silence.
Joey looks at his hand and says, "That's ridiculous."
Ross closely examines his own fingers.
"Can I use either thumb?" Ross asks, looking up.
Chandler lowers his foot.
"I'd rather go with the foot theory," Chandler says while looking down. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel approaches the group carrying a tray of drinks.
"All right, don't tell me, don't tell me," Rachel says as she stops near the table.
Rachel looks down at the tray, examining the cups.
Rachel picks up a mug and hands it to Ross, saying, "Decaf cappuccino for Joey."
Ross holds the cup as Rachel reaches for the next drink.
Rachel hands another mug to Chandler, saying, "Coffee black,"
Chandler takes the mug.
"late," Rachel adds, handing a pink cup to Joey.
Joey takes the pink cup while Rachel reaches for the last drink.
Rachel hands a tall glass to Monica, saying, "and an iced tea I gave it pretty good."
"Excellent," Joey says.
Rachel smiles.
"Good for me," Rachel says, gesturing with her empty tray. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel turns and walks away. Joey, Ross, Chandler, and Monica quickly pass their drinks around to exchange them with one another.
They finish swapping the drinks just as Phoebe enters the frame and walks toward the empty spot on the couch next to Ross.
Phoebe sits down, dropping her bag onto her lap, and exhales as Ross looks at her.
Phoebe waves her hands dismissively and says, "Okay, baby. Yeah, no, I'm just it's not it's not even worth it's. My bank."
Ross leans forward and asks, "What did they do to you?"
Phoebe shifts her posture.
Phoebe gestures as she says, "Okay, I'm going through my mail and I open up their monthly statements."
"Easy," Ross says, holding up a hand.
Phoebe continues gesturing excitedly.
"And there's 500 extra dollars in my account," Phoebe says.
Chandler leans forward and says, "Oh, Satan's minions at work again." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe looks toward Chandler.
Phoebe replies, "Yes, because now I have to go down there and deal with them." Joey looks at her and says, "What are you talking about? Keep it."
"It's not mine. I didn't earn it. If I kept it, it would be like stealing," Phoebe says.
Rachel approaches from behind the couch and says, "Yeah, but if you spent it, it would be like shopping." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe looks at Rachel, then picks up her yellow mug.
"Okay?" Phoebe says, holding the mug.
"Let's say I bought a really great pair of shoes. Do you know what I'd hear with every step I took?" Phoebe asks, gesturing with her free hand.
Phoebe mimics walking with her hand and says at a rhythmic pace, "Not mine. Not mine. Not mine."
Setting her mug down, Phoebe mimics skipping with her hands and says, "And even if I was happy, okay, and skipping, I'd hear, not mine. Not mine." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica raises her hand toward Phoebe.
Monica gestures gently and says, "We're with you. We got it. Okay."
Phoebe continues, "I'd never be able to enjoy it. It would be like this giant karmic debt." Chandler leans forward behind her.
Monica looks at Chandler, who is leaning forward behind Phoebe, and asks, "Chandler, what are you doing?"
Chandler pulls back.
Monica grabs Chandler's arm and says, "Hey, what are you doing?"
Chandler exhales a cloud of smoke, revealing a cigarette in his hand. Audience laughter is audible.
The group collectively groans, "Oh," expressing dismay.
The group waves their hands to clear the smoke.
"What is this?" Monica asks. Chandler gestures with the cigarette and says, "I'm smoking. I'm smoking."
Monica replies, "Oh, I can't believe you. You've been so good for three years." Chandler holds up the cigarette and adds, "And this is my reward."
Ross leans forward.
Ross gestures and says, "Hold on a second, all right? Just think about what you went through the last time you quit. You know what? Forget"
Ross continues, "about you. Think about what we went through the last time you quit." Chandler takes a drag from the cigarette and says, "Okay, so this time I won't quit."
The group talks over each other in protest as Chandler waves his hand defensively.
"I'm putting it out," Chandler says, reaching forward.
Chandler drops the cigarette.
"Oh, no," Phoebe says, looking down at her mug as Chandler drops the cigarette into it.
Phoebe stares at the mug.
"I can't drink this now," Phoebe says. Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe continues to look at her ruined drink.
In a coffee shop, Joey, Ross, Chandler, and others sit at tables and armchairs while Rachel and Phoebe stand behind the sofa. Monica walks over, wiping her hands together. Audience laughter fades.
Monica stops by the sofa and says, "All right, I'm going to go change. I've got a date." Rachel leans forward and asks, "Just Alan again?"
Monica turns her attention toward Ross.
Ross, holding a mug near his mouth, asks, "How's it going?"
Monica gestures with her hands and says, "Going pretty good, you know? It's nice. We're having fun."
Joey asks, "So when do we get to meet the guy?" Rachel adds, "Yeah."
Monica briefly lowers her gaze, then says, "Say today's Monday."
"Never," Monica says, her pitch flat. Audience laughter is audible.
The group reacts simultaneously with overlapping groans and exclamations.
Monica points downward and says, "Not after what happened with Steve." Chandler, sitting on an armchair, replies, "What are you talking about? We love Steve."
"Steve was sexy," Chandler adds, his pitch dropping noticeably on the last word.
Monica stares silently at Chandler and points at him. Audience laughter continues.
Monica lowers her hand. "Sorry. Look, I don't even know how I feel about him yet."
"Just give me a chance to figure that out," Monica says. Rachel asks, "Well, then can we meet him?"
Rachel holds her hands together, waiting for a response.
"No, sorry," Monica replies, shaking her head.
Joey points two fingers at his own neck while looking toward Ross.
Ross points back at Joey. "Joey. Joey, let it-" Ross says, waving his hand.
The setting changes to the exterior of a restaurant at night; a taxi drives past. The setting then changes to an indoor kitchen where Monica and Paula wear white chef uniforms and hats.
Monica says, "mean. Why should I let them meet him? I mean, I bring a guy home and within five minutes they're all over him." Paula chops vegetables. Monica continues, "I mean, they're like"
"...coyotes picking off the weak members of the herd," Monica says, gesturing with her hand.
Paula pauses her chopping and turns her head toward Monica.
"Listen, as someone who's seen more than her share of bad beef I'll tell you," Paula says, raising her hands.
"that is not such a terrible thing," Paula says, holding her knife. She resumes chopping and adds, "Come on now, though, your friends are just looking out after you."
Monica gestures with her hand as she says, "I just wish that once I'd bring a guy"
Paula looks at Monica.
"...home they actually liked," Monica says.
Paula turns to Monica. "Well, you do realize that the odds of that happening are a little slimmer if they never get to meet the"
Monica nods. "know. I know. Do you mind if I whimper a little bit?"
Monica makes soft whimpering sounds while Paula stares at her.
Monica stops whimpering and says, "Okay, I'm done."
The setting changes to Monica's apartment. Joey sits on an armchair eating from a plate, and Ross sits across from him.
Joey says, "Let it go, Ross." Ross looks at Joey, holding a small dark item, and says, "Yeah, well, you didn't know Chi Chi."
Joey takes a bite of food. The setting changes to the apartment living room at night. Ross, Joey, Phoebe, and Rachel sit on the furniture while Monica stands near the doorway.
"You all promise?" Monica asks. The group responds together, "Yeah, we promise." "We'll be good."
Monica points toward the window. "Chandler, do you promise to be good?"
Through the rain-streaked window, Chandler stands outside on the balcony, pressing his face against the glass.
Chandler slides sideways along the glass, then taps on the window pane.
Joey calls out toward the window, "You can come in, but your filter-tipped little buddy has to stay outside."
Outside the window, Chandler raises his hands as rain falls on him. Audience laughter continues.
The front door opens and Phoebe walks in, holding a bag and some mail. She approaches the sofa.
Phoebe looks at a piece of mail and reads, "Dear Ms."
"...Buffet, thank you for calling attention to our error. We have credited your account to $500. We're sorry for the" Phoebe continues reading.
"...inconvenience and hope you'll accept this football phone," Phoebe says as she pulls a football-shaped phone from her bag.
Monica, Rachel, Joey, and Ross look at Phoebe in silence. Audience laughter is audible.
"...as our free gift. Do you believe it?" Phoebe asks, holding up the phone.
Phoebe looks around at the others. "Now I have $1,000 and a football phone."
Rachel stands up from her chair and walks over to Phoebe.
"What bank is this?" Rachel asks, taking the letter from Phoebe's hand.
Rachel examines the letter.
A buzzer sounds. Monica turns toward the door. "Okay,"
Monica walks to the intercom on the wall beside the door and presses a button.
"Who is it?" Monica asks into the intercom. "Alan," a voice replies. Chandler, now inside and wet, walks past. "Chandler. He's here," Monica says.
Chandler stands near the doorway, shaking water out of his hair.
Monica turns to Ross and gestures to her dress. "Do I look okay?"
Ross points at her neckline. "One more button."
Monica buttons the top of her dress. "Okay, close. How about closed?" she asks.
Ross looks at Monica.
Ross says, "I didn't know."
Monica touches her hair and flutters her hands near her face.
Monica holds her hands up, palms open. "Please be good. Do you remember how much you all like me?"
Monica opens the front door and steps out into the hallway. The rest of the group waits in the living room.
Monica re-enters, leading Alan inside. "Hi, Alan. This is everybody. Everybody, this is"
The group stares toward the door. Audience laughter is audible.
In an apartment, Monica stands next to a man, Alan, near the open front door.
Monica turns her head toward the living room and says, "Alan."
Alan smiles and steps slightly forward.
Alan says, "I've heard so much about all these"
In the living room, Chandler, Joey, Ross, Rachel, and Phoebe laugh and smile toward the door. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to a view of a city skyline at night with illuminated skyscrapers.
Back at the apartment, Alan stands in the hallway facing Monica and says, "I'll call you,"
Alan turns and walks down the hallway. Monica smiles and holds the door open.
Alan says, "okay?"
Monica keeps her hand on the doorframe.
Monica says, "Okay."
Monica closes the door, turns toward the living room, and says, "Let's see. Alan bashing begin."
Monica begins walking toward the living room.
Monica approaches the group and asks, "Who's going to take the first shot?"
Phoebe and Chandler look forward in silence.
Monica says, "Come on."
Ross looks down at a small object in his hands.
Ross looks up and says, "I'll go."
Monica crosses her arms. Ross clears his throat.
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "Let's start with the way he kept picking at you know I'm sorry, I can't do this."
Ross tosses the object down and says, "Can't do it. We loved him." The rest of the group suddenly cheers and talks over each other.
The group continues cheering. Joey and Chandler stand up, and Rachel smiles.
Monica gestures with her hands and says, "Wait a minute. We're talking about someone that I am going out with."
Phoebe nods and speaks indistinctly over the group's voices.
Phoebe says, "That pimento trick." Chandler adds, "I will never look at an olive the same way again."
Ross raises his hands, palms up.
Rachel points to herself and says, "And did you notice?"
Joey points forward and says, "Yeah," overlapping with Rachel's line.
The group murmurs in agreement. Monica stands with her arms crossed, looking at them.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "no, it was great. The way his smile was kind of crooked."
Phoebe points forward and says, "Yes, like the man in the shoe." Ross asks, "What shoe?"
Phoebe turns her head toward Ross.
Phoebe says, "From the nursery rhyme. There was a crooked man who had a crooked smile"
Phoebe continues, "who lived in his shoe for a while"
The group stares silently at Phoebe. Monica lowers her head slightly.
Ross stands up and walks toward the kitchen area, saying, "so I think Alan will become the yardstick against which all future boyfriends will be measured."
Rachel turns to Monica and says, "What future"
Rachel continues, "boyfriends? No, no. I think this could know." Monica asks, "Really?" Rachel replies, "Oh, yeah."
Chandler gestures with his hand and says, "I'd marry him just for his David Hasselhoff impression alone."
Joey throws his head back and laughs.
Joey points at Chandler and says, "You know I'm going to be doing that at parties, right?"
Chandler does a small dancing motion with his arms. Ross holds a bottle in the kitchen.
Ross holds the bottle and says, "You know what I like most about him, though?" Monica asks, "What?"
Ross points a finger slightly upward.
Ross says, "The way he makes me feel about myself."
The group sits in silence, their expressions remaining still.
Monica lowers her gaze, turns slightly, and stands in place.
The setting changes to the exterior of a coffee shop. Text on the window reads "CENTRAL PERK". People are visible inside.
Inside Central Perk, Monica sits at the counter reading a magazine.
Ross, Joey, Chandler, and Rachel enter the coffee shop carrying softball equipment. Monica turns to look at them.
Monica asks, "How was the game?"
The group stops near the couch.
Chandler says, "Well,"
The group cheers loudly, dropping a pom-pom and tossing their gear onto the couch.
Monica says, "fantastic. I have one question. How is that possible?"
Ross points forward and steps toward Monica.
Ross gestures with his hand and says, "Alan, he was unbelievable."
Ross continues, "He was like that Bugs Bunny cartoon where Bugs is playing all the positions,"
Ross says, "right? But instead of Bugs, it was first base, Alan."
Ross shifts his posture and says, "Second base, Alan."
The screen fades to black.
The screen remains black.
